message.content: Adversaries may compromise third-party infrastructure, including physical or cloud servers, domains, and web/DNS services, to support their operations. This tactic allows them to stage, launch, and execute attacks while blending in with normal traffic, making detection and attribution challenging. Common methods include forming botnets and leveraging compromised infrastructure for phishing campaigns and information gathering, often using digital certificates to enhance legitimacy. The impact includes increased difficulty in tracing malicious activities back to the adversary and potential misuse of trusted sites. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring for unusual activity, implementing robust access controls, and employing threat intelligence to identify and respond to compromised infrastructure.
